mnemonics: TYPE & FIND ROOT: 'T' for 'type', 'F' for 'find', 'R' for 'root' to remember the three main command categories and the special superuser.
keyPoints: Use 'type' to determine if a command is a shell built-in, alias, or external binary (e.g., `type cd`).
Use 'which' or the more robust 'command -v' to locate the path of an external executable command.
The 'root' user (UID 0) is the superuser with unrestricted system access, typically logged into for administrative tasks only.
commonMistakes: Confusing 'type' (displays command information) with 'which' (only finds path of external commands).
Assuming 'which' will reliably find all commands; it doesn't work for shell built-ins or aliases, unlike 'type' or 'command -v'.
